VESSEL HAS THE LIBERTY TO USE MGO FOR MAIN ENGINE/GENERATOR ENGINES WHEN MANOEUVRING IN/OUT OF PORTS OR IN NARROW/SHALLOW WATERS, CANALS, RIVERS, IN/OUT PORT, SHIFTING OF GENERATOR ENGINE, WHEN BALLASTING / DEBALLASTING, AND STARTING UP/TURNING OFF ENGINES.
ABOVE SPEED AND CONSUMPTION FIGURES ARE ON THE BASIS OF A CLEAN HULL, ONLY APPLY ON GOOD WEATHER DAYS (OF A MINIMUM 24 CONSECUTIVE HOURS) AND ANY OF THE FOLLOWING DAYS ARE EXCLUDED FROM ANY WARRANTY AS TO THE VESSEL$B!G(BS SPEED AND PERFORMANCE: THOSE IN WHICH THE WEATHER CONDITIONS EXCEED EITHER BEAUFORT FORCE 4 AND/OR DOUGLAS SEA STATE 3 AND SIGNIFICANT WAVE AND SWELL HEIGHT OF 1.25M OR GREATER OR THOSE IN WHICH THERE ARE NEGATIVE INFLUENCES OF SWELL OR THOSE IN WHICH THERE ARE ADVERSE CURRENTS.
ALL DETAILS $B!H(BABOUT$B!I(B WHICH IS UNDERSTOOD TO MEAN A 0.5 KNOTS TOLERANCE AND A 5% ALLOWANCE ON BUNKER CONSUMPTION.  IN THE EVENT THAT ANY SPEED/ PERFORMANCE CLAIM IS MADE, OWNERS ARE ENTITLED TO OFFSET THE VALUE OF ANY BUNKER SAVINGS MADE BY CHARTERERS BELOW THE UPPER LIMIT OF THE WARRANTED CONSUMPTION.  POSITIVE CURRENTS NOT TO BE CONSIDERED FOR ANALYSING PERFORMANCE.
ALL BUNKERS SUPPLIED TO THE VESSEL TO CONFORM WITH INTERNATIONAL INTERMEDIATE FUEL OIL ISO 8217:2010(E), CLASS RMG380 FOR I.F.O. 380 CST AND ISO 8217:2010(E), DMA FOR M.G.O. IN SOUTH AFRICA, IF RMG380 GRADE OF FUEL OIL IS NOT AVAILABLE, CHARTERERS SHALL HAVE THE LIBERTY TO SUPPLY RMG180 GRADE OF FUEL OIL (180CST)
